summary: David's final days give way to Solomon's anointing and the swift consolidation of his kingdom, while three wisdom psalms call God's people to trust, endure in old age, and rest in divine justice.
psalm_rows_html:
PsalmTypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
37Wisdom / AcrosticDavidObserving the prosperity of the wicked while the righteous sufferDo not fret over evildoers; delight in the LORD and trust his timingv.4 "Delight yourself in the LORD, and he will give you the desires of your heart."Jesus quotes v.11 in the Beatitudes: "the meek shall inherit the earth" (Matt 5:5)
71Lament / TrustDavid (implied)Old age and declining strength; enemies who say "God has forsaken him"Lifelong faith: the God of youth is still refuge in old agev.9 "Do not cast me off in the time of old age; forsake me not when my strength is spent."Reflects the believer's confidence in God's faithfulness through every season of life (Rom 8:38-39)
94Lament / RoyalUnknownOppression by wicked rulers who crush God's people and say "the LORD does not see"God the righteous Judge sees, disciplines, and ultimately vindicates his peoplev.12-13 "Blessed is the man whom you discipline, O LORD... to give him rest from days of trouble."Paul quotes v.11 in 1 Cor 3:20: "The Lord knows the thoughts of the wise, that they are futile."
